Literati Treasures · Traceable Craftsmanship

Small Wonders,
Vast Heritage

Wen Gu Hall curates jade bracelets, Buddhist amulets, Yixing zisha teaware, Chinese ink paintings and calligraphy — each piece traceable to its maker, sourced directly from artisans and collectors, bringing centuries of craft into everyday life.

About Wen Gu Hall

We believe true elegance lives in texture and patina. Every jade bead, every amulet, every zisha pot, every scroll of painting or calligraphy carries years of an artisan's devoted work.

Featured Pieces

Twenty-two signature works spanning jade bracelets, jade pendants, zisha teaware, ink painting and calligraphy.
Our Story

From a Scholar's Studio
to Wen Gu Hall

In 2011, founder Chen Mo wandered the back alleys of Suzhou and, in a small zisha workshop, first felt the warmth of a fully hand-thrown teapot. Over the following decade he traveled to Hotan, Chiang Mai, Yixing, Yangzhou and Huizhou, seeking out the artisans and collectors who still work entirely by hand.

Wen Gu Hall still authenticates every piece individually and sources directly from origin, with no assembly-line reproductions. We maintain long-term partnerships with over twenty jade carvers, temple communities, zisha artisans and painters — every item traceable to its place of origin and its maker.
